content.problemStatement:
JJ has an array AAA of length NNN. He can perform the following operation on AAA:


Select any LLL, RRR (1≤L≤R≤N1 \le L \le R \le N1≤L≤R≤N) and for each iii such that L≤i≤RL \le i \le RL≤i≤R, set Ai:=Ai+1A_i := A_i + 1Ai​:=Ai​+1.
JJ wants to make AAA palindromic. Find the **minimum** number of moves to do so.

Note: An array is called a palindrome if it reads the same backwards and forwards, for e.g. [1,3,3,1][1, 3, 3, 1][1,3,3,1] and [6,2,6][6, 2, 6][6,2,6] are palindromic.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum number of moves to make AAA palindromic.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: We can perform the following operations:

[2,6,4,3,4,1‾]→L=3,R=6[2,6,5,4,5,2][2, 6, \underline{4, 3, 4, 1}] \xrightarrow{L = 3, R = 6} [2, 6, 5, 4, 5, 2][2,6,4,3,4,1​]L=3,R=6​[2,6,5,4,5,2]
[2,6,5,4,5‾,2]→L=4,R=5[2,6,5,5,6,2][2, 6, 5, \underline{4, 5}, 2] \xrightarrow{L = 4, R = 5} [2, 6, 5, 5, 6, 2][2,6,5,4,5​,2]L=4,R=5​[2,6,5,5,6,2]

Test Case 2: We perform the operation on (L=1,R=1)(L = 1, R = 1)(L=1,R=1) 999 times after which array becomes [10,10][10, 10][10,10].
Test Case 3: AAA is already a palindrome.
